heading: Establishment of product categories for short life cycle merchandise
label: 1673h Establishment of product categories for short life cycle merchandise
notes: References in TextThe Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(B)(v), is not set out in the Code. See Publication of Harmonized Tariff Schedule note set out under .section 1202 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible domestic entity may file a petition with the Commission requesting that a product category be established with respect to short life cycle merchandise at any time after the merchandise becomes the subject of 2 or more affirmative dumping determinations.

children.label: i
children.text: identify the short life cycle merchandise that is the subject of the affirmative dumping determinations,

children.label: ii
children.text: specify the short life cycle merchandise that the petitioner seeks to have included in the same product category as the merchandise that is subject to the affirmative dumping determinations,

children.label: iii
children.text: specify any short life cycle merchandise the petitioner particularly seeks to have excluded from the product category,

children.label: iv
children.text: provide reasons for the inclusions and exclusions specified under clauses (ii) and (iii), and

children.label: v
children.text: identify such merchandise in terms of the designations used in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: request the administering authority to confirm promptly the affirmative determinations on which the petition is based, and

children.label: B
children.text: upon receipt of such confirmation, determine whether the merchandise covered by the confirmed affirmative determinations is short life cycle merchandise and whether the petitioner is an eligible domestic entity.

children.label: A
children.text: publish notice in the Federal Register that the petition has been received, and

children.label: B
children.text: provide opportunity for the presentation of views regarding the establishment of the requested product category, including a public hearing if requested by any interested person.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: By no later than the date that is 90 days after the date on which a petition is filed under paragraph (1), the Commission shall determine the scope of the product category into which the short life cycle merchandise that is the subject of the affirmative dumping determinations identified in such petition shall be classified for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Modifications not requested by petition
children.label: B Modifications not requested by petition

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Commission may, on its own initiative, make a determination modifying the scope of any product category established under subparagraph (A) at any time.

children.label: I
children.text: published in the Federal Register notice of the proposed modification, and

children.label: II
children.text: provided interested parties an opportunity for a hearing, and a period for the submission of written comments, on the classification of merchandise into the product categories to be affected by such determination.

children.heading: Basis of determinations
children.label: C Basis of determinations
children.text: In making determinations under subparagraph (A) or (B), the Commission shall ensure that each product category consists of similar short life cycle merchandise which is produced by similar processes under similar circumstances and has similar uses.

children.label: A
children.text: like or directly competitive with other merchandise that is the subject of 2 or more affirmative dumping determinations, or

children.label: B
children.text: is similar enough to such other merchandise as to be considered for inclusion with such merchandise in a product monitoring category established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: any affirmative final determination made by the administering authority under during the 8-year period preceding the filing of the petition under this section that results in the issuance of an antidumping duty order under which requires the deposit of estimated antidumping duties at a rate of not less than 15 percent ad valorem, orsection 1673d(a) of this titlesection 1673e of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is made by the administering authority under during the 8-year period preceding the filing of the petition under this section in the course of an investigation for which no final determination is made under by reason of a suspension of the investigation under , andsection 1673b(b) of this titlesection 1673d of this titlesection 1673c of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: includes a determination that the estimated average amount by which the normal value of the merchandise exceeds the export price (or the constructed export price) of the merchandise is not less than 15 percent ad valorem.

children.label: i
children.text: makes a separate determination of the amount by which the normal value of such merchandise of the manufacturer exceeds the export price (or the constructed export price) of such merchandise of the manufacturer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: specifically identifies the manufacturer by name with such amount in the affirmative dumping determination or in an antidumping duty order issued as a result of the affirmative dumping determination.

children.label: i
children.text: such merchandise of the manufacturer is part of a group of merchandise to which the administering authority assigns (in lieu of making separate determinations described in subparagraph (A)(i)(I)) an amount determined to be the amount by which the normal value of the merchandise in such group exceeds the export price (or the constructed export price) of the merchandise in such group, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the merchandise and the manufacturer are not specified by name in the affirmative dumping determination or in any antidumping duty order issued as a result of such affirmative dumping determination.

children.heading: Short life cycle merchandise
children.label: 4 Short life cycle merchandise
children.text: The term “short life cycle merchandise” means any product that the Commission determines is likely to become outmoded within 4 years, by reason of technological advances, after the product is commercially available. For purposes of this paragraph, the term “outmoded” refers to a kind of style that is no longer state-of-the-art.

For purposes of this section and section 1673b(b)(1)(B) and (C) of this title, all affirmative dumping determinations described in subsection (b)(2)(A) that were made after , and before , and all affirmative dumping determinations described in subsection (b)(2)(B) that were made after , and before , with respect to each category of short life cycle merchandise of the same manufacturer shall be treated as one affirmative dumping determination with respect to that category for that manufacturer which was made on the date on which the latest of such determinations was made.December 31, 1980August 23, 1988December 31, 1984August 23, 1988

children.label: A
children.text: is described in subsection (b)(2)(A) and was made before , orJanuary 1, 1981

children.label: B
children.text: is described in subsection (b)(2)(B) and was made before ,January 1, 1985
